original: The Wright brothers attempt to fly the Wright Flyer for the first time at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina
NorwegianRoald Amundsen's Antarctic expedition is the first to reach the South Pole
Amazon announces its 1st delivery by drone 2 km from their warehouse in the UK
University of Toronto scientists present findings of the world's oldest water - 2 billion years old from a mine in Canada
DNA created in a test tube
Mariner 2 makes 1st US fly-by of another planet (Venus)
Experimental airplane Voyager, piloted by Dick Rutan & Jeana Yeager, takes off from Edwards AFB, California on 1st non-stop, non-refueled flight around the world; lands 9 days later
Eugene Cernan & Harrison Schmitt leave the Moon
Total solar eclipse visible across southern Chile and Argentina
Britt Allcroft, British film and television producer and writer who created the children's TV series "Thomas the Tank Engine & Friends", born in Worthing, West Sussex, England
Steph Curry makes his 2,974th three-point shot to pass Ray Allen as the NBA's greatest career 3-point shooter during Golden State Warriors' 105-96 win over NY Knicks at Madison Square Garden
The Millau viaduct, the highest bridge in the world, designed by architect Norman Foster and engineer Michel Virlogeux, near Millau, France is officially opened
Hans von Ohain, German-American aerospace engineer, developed 1st turbojet engine, born in Dessau, Duchy of Anhalt German Empire (d. 1998)[1]
Birth of Quantum Physics: German Physicist Max Planck presents a theoretical derivation of his black-body radiation law
The Vatican announces it has rediscovered the lost last paintings ofRaphaelin Vatican Museum, painted in 1520
The Walt Disney Company buys most of 21st Century Fox for $52.4 billion
"Star Wars: The Force Awakens" directed byJ.J. Abramsand starringDaisy Ridley,John BoyegaandHarrison Fordhas its world premiere at the TCL Chinese Theatre in Los Angeles
Construction begins on the Three Gorges Dam, China
Artificial pearls 1st manufactured by M. Jacquin in Paris made of gypsum pellets covered with fish scales
The Forty-seven Ronin (leaderless samurai), under the command of Ōishi Kuranosuke, avenge the death of their master in Japan
Jackson Five make their 1st appearance on "TheEd SullivanShow"
Catherine Coleman, American USAF officer and astronaut (STS 73), born in Charleston, South Carolina
Carl Scheer, American basketball executive (general manager Denver Nuggets, LA Clippers, Charlotte Hornets; inventor NBA Slam Dunk Contest), born in Springfield, Massachusetts (d. 2019)
Morihei Ueshiba, Japanese martial artist and founder of the Japanese martial art of aikido, born in Tanabe, Wakayama, Japan (d. 1969)
Joe B. Jordan in F-104C starfighter sets world altitude record, 31,513m
Australia v West Indies 1st Test Cricket at the Gabba ends in a tie
Miss World won by Miss Jamaica Toni-Ann Singh in London. First time all five major beauty titles held by black women.
HSBC, Europe's largest bank, says it will stop financing new oil and gas fields, in attempt to lower global greenhouse gas emissions
Cyclone Chido makes a direct hit on the French island territory of Mayotte off the coast of east Africa before striking northern Mozambique
First incident of American Revolution - 400 New Hampshire militiamen successfully attack Fort William and Mary
Egyptian & Israeli representatives gather in Cairo for first formal peace conference
UN General Assembly establishes High Commission for Refugees, goes on to win Nobel Peace Prize in 1954
John Mercer Langston, 1st African-American to hold US political office, born in Louisa County, Virginia (d. 1897)
Glenda E. Gray, South African scientist specializing in HIV research, born in Boksburg, South Africa
Robert Alan Ridley Parker, American PhD/astronaut (STS-9, STS-35), born in New York City
Steve MacLean, Canadian astronaut (STS 52), born in Ottawa, Ontario
Edward L Tatum, American molecular geneticist and biochemist (Nobel Prize 1958), born in Boulder, Colorado (d. 1975)
Spike Jones [Lindley Armstrong Jones], American bandleader ("Der Fueher's Face", "Cocktails For Two"), born in Long Beach, California (d. 1965)
Craig Biggio, American Baseball HOF infielder (7 × MLB All-Star; 4 × Gold Glove Award; 5 × Silver Slugger Award; Houston Astros), born in Smithtown, New York
Bill Buckner, American MLB 1st baseman (1986 World Series infamous fielding error; NL batting champ 1980), born in Vallejo, California (d. 2019)
Bill Ranford, Canadian ice hockey goaltender (Stanley Cup 1988, 90 Edmonton Oilers; Conn Smythe Trophy 1990; Canada Cup MVP 1991), born in Brandon, Manitoba
Bobby Orrscores first career hat trick (vs Chicago Blackhawks)
LA Kings'Marcel Dionnebecomes 9th NHLer to score 500 goals
Ruth Fuchs, German javelin thrower (Olympic 2 gold-72, 76), born in Egeln, Sachsen-Anhalt, Germany
Siiri Rantanen, Finnish cross country skier (Olympic gold 3 × 5k relay 1956, bronze 1960; bronze 10k 1952), born in Tohmajärvi, North Karelia, Finland (d. 2023)
Cliff Williams, English rock bassist (AC/DC), born in Romford, Essex, England
Beth Orton, English folk-electronica singer and songwriter (Central Reservation), born in East Dereham, Norfolk, England
Peter "Spider" Tracy, English rock tin whistle player and vocalist (The Pogues - "Fairy Tale Of New York"), born in Eastbourne, England
John Lurie, American jazz saxophonist (Lounge Lizards), composer and actor (Down By Law; Stranger Than Paradise), born in Minneapolis, Minnesota
Dandy Livingstone (Robert Livingstone Thompson), Jamaican-British reggae musician and record producer ("Suzanne Beware of the Devil"; "Rudy, A Message to You"), born in Kingston, Jamaica
Raj Kapoor, Indian actor known as "the greatest showman of Hindi cinema" (Awaara, Boot Polish), born in Peshawar, British India (d. 1988)
Clark Terry, American jazz trumpeter, born in St Louis, Missouri (d. 2015)
Vijay Amritraj, Indian tennis player and actor (Octopussy), born in Chennai, India
Dee Wallace Stone [Deanna Bowers], American actress (Critters, 10, ET), born in Kansas City, Kanasas
Aphra Behn, English playwright and novelist (Oroonoko - one of earliest English novels), baptized in Canterbury, England (d. 1689)
Helen Blair Bartlett, American geologist credited with inventing the insulation for spark plugs, born in Sharpsville, Pennsylvania (d. 1969)
B.K.S. Iyengar, Indian yoga advocate, born in Bellur, Kolar district, Kingdom of Mysore (d. 2014)
Sam Jones, American baseball pitcher (MLB All-Star 1955, 59; no-hitter 1955; Chicago Cubs), born in Stewartsville, Ohio (d. 1971)
Jaun Elia, Pakistani scholar, poet and philosopher, born in Amroha, Uttar Pradesh, British India (d. 2002)
Leo Wright, American jazz saxophone and flute player (Dizzy Gillespie;; Blues Shout; I Left My Heart In San Francisco), born in Wichita Falls, Texas (d. 1991)
